body:

---
Peter,

I wanted to introduce myself: I'm Bettina, and I'm Abigail's Campaign Manager!

I'm so excited to be back on the team so we can hit the ground running with Abigail's re-election. I've had the chance to meet a lot of our supporters out in all 10 counties in VA-07 during the last campaign, and I look forward to meeting more of you!

Driving from Culpeper to Goochland or Henrico to Nottoway, and everywhere in between has put a lot of miles on my car, given me the opportunity to meet amazing people from across Central Virginia, and get to know this beautiful area of Virginia. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/Ylsef9NmqHzSAMZctJXLg2X45JzfIXXO6du81e2OMCY=/mDuN2W4M79ACu15RUfdROM7BmiSzlrZWhlYLbv81xrA=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20190714-1-EM&amount=25) _Here 's a picture of us after Abigail's debate in 2018!_ When I joined the campaign, Abigail and I talked a lot about the challenges we're likely to face. We know that we're going to see significant spending from Super PACs against us because they want to see VA-07 in Republican control again. Because we don't accept corporate PAC money, we need to rely on grassroots support.

But as always, I know Abigail is up to the challenge, and so am I.

We want to let you know what Elizabeth's been up to this past week -- hosting public events in four states -- but first, do you have a few seconds to read about why it's possible?

>
Elizabeth started out in Houston last weekend, talking to educators about strong public schools. (Fun fact: Did you know that when she was a commuter student at the University of Houston, tuition was $50 a semester? Now she's fighting to make sure more Americans get opportunities like that -- with her plans for universal free public college and cancelling student loan debt).

Then Elizabeth headed over to New Orleans to talk about her plans to make sure women of color get a fair shot. She got asked to give some advice to her 25-year-old self (get in the fight!), addressed an overflow crowd in Peterborough, NH, and met Wisconsinites in Milwaukee and Madison.

At every one of these events, Elizabeth's doing her signature selfie line. She's taken more than 36,000 selfies so far and she's making sure everyone who wants a picture gets one.

It's not just about the selfies. It's about taking the time to really meet voters -- one on one, face to face, family to family. That's how we're building this campaign, and that's how we make it stronger every day.

Team Warren

name: Cory Booker
subject: Jaime Harrison
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Friend, if you were stranded on a deserted island, what would you bring with you? If you could pick a superpower what would it be? What's the one thing you would fix in this world if you could wave a wand and make it happen? These are the types of questions I'd routinely ask my friend and classmate, Jaime Harrison, in the dining hall of our college dorm. I wish you could have heard his answers because they played a big part in my knowing that this guy is special -- that he is deeply committed to helping people. I've known Jaime for a long time and am lucky to call him a friend. He didn't grow up with much but was determined not to let that slow him down. He worked hard, became the first person in his family to graduate college, and self-started his career. And through it all, he became a leader in the community that supported him. **Jaime Harrison is running to defeat Lindsey Graham for Senate in South Carolina.** [_**We need a progressive champion like Jaime in the Senate -- join me in chipping in any amount to help Jaime have the resources he 'll need to win.**_](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-harrison?refcode=em_fr_20190714_CB_20190714-fr-CB-harrisonsplit-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Lindsey Graham is one of Trump's closest allies in the Senate. He voted against the Affordable Care Act and instead stood behind repealing coverage for almost 20 million people. He became the GOP's spokesman for confirming Brett Kavanaugh to the Supreme Court and dismissed Christine Blasey Ford's powerful and heart-wrenching testimony. If you want to fight back, and to send Trump a message, here's your chance. Jaime's running because no family should have to choose between paying the electric bill and putting food on the table. He's running to make sure that every one of his constituents has their voice represented in Washington. Jaime is exactly who South Carolinians need in their corner. And team, Jaime can win this race against Lindsey Graham. But he'll need our help. [_**Chip in whatever you can to give South Carolina and all Americans a senator who will put people over party. Donate now to send Jaime to the Senate.**_](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-harrison?refcode=em_fr_20190714_CB_20190714-fr-CB-harrisonsplit-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Thanks, Cory
[](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-harrison?refcode=em_fr_20190714_CB_20190714-fr-CB-harrisonsplit-actives_actives_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

name: Rob Flaherty, Beto for America
subject: see you there?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
We’ve got a big debate coming up. Donate any amount and you could win a trip for two to join Beto.
#
We’ve got a big debate coming up at the end of the month. It’s our chance to talk about the greatest set of challenges this country has ever faced and how we’re going to overcome each and every single one of them. Beto has two extra tickets so we’re officially bringing one lucky supporter (and a friend) with us to Detroit -- and I know Beto would love to have you there in his corner.

subject: I've got a message for anyone in the Trump administration who needs to hear it:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
In a Warren administration, I'll launch a task force in the Department of Justice to investigate the Trump administration's criminal abuses at the border, and we'll hold perpetrators accountable. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852efb6/45c30650/14b09fb35/669c9363/263466363/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
I've got a message for anyone in the Trump administration who needs to hear it:

When you physically abuse immigrants, sexually abuse immigrants, or refuse to provide them the medical care that they need, you break the law of the United States of America.

Donald Trump may be willing to look the other way.

But President Elizabeth Warren will not.

In a Warren administration, I'll launch a task force in the Department of Justice to investigate the Trump administration's criminal abuses at the border, and we'll hold perpetrators accountable.

**[Will you add your name to make it clear that we can't shy away from holding the Trump administration accountable for their abuses?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852efb6/45c30650/14b09fb35/669c9362/263466363/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDcxNGVtMi1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-hold-trump-accountable?source=20190714em2-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

When people come here who are desperate -- people who come here whose lives have been turned upside down, people who come here to try to build a better future -- then we need to treat them with humanity, and we need to follow the law.

We're going to fight against these human rights violations with everything that we've got. And we won't ever back down.

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

* * *
**Quick fact check:** Water and air quality under Donald Trump is actually getting **_worse_** after YEARS of improvement because of the extreme policies promoted by the Trump administration.
But Trump went beyond merely lying at the international G-20 summit to defend his obstruction on climate change -- He denied basic scientific principles himself.
Trump tried to justify continuing to invest in super-polluting coal plants and offshore drilling platforms instead of renewable energy sources by saying that wind power **" doesn't work"** when... 'the wind turns off.'
He said investments in solar aren't profitable (which they are!) because ... 'sometimes it's cloudy.'
That isn't how renewable energy works at all, but that's besides the point. Trump is making the United States an **international pariah on climate change** and wasting what little time we have to address the climate crisis with these scientifically illiterate remarks.
No more inaction. No more obstruction. No more lies and excuses. [**Call on the Trump administration to work alongside international leaders to address climate change ->**](https://secure.314action.org/page/m/29bb46cf/4bd7760e/36d05e20/77e7a37d/2907136157/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "https://secure.314action.org/page/s/314_EM_EN_190714_G20Climate_U3_X3?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=naughton&utm_content=4+-+Call+on+the+Trump+administration+to+work&utm_campaign=314_EM_EN_190714_G20Climate_U3_X3&source=314_EM_EN_190714_G20Climate_U3_X3")
